3	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
4	import copy
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from enum import Enum, IntEnum
7	from functools import cached_property
8	from typing import Annotated, Any, Optional, Union
9	
10	import msgspec
11	from pydantic import BaseModel
12	from typing_extensions import deprecated
13	
14	from vllm.logger import init_logger
15	from vllm.logits_process import LogitsProcessor
16	from vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer import AnyTokenizer
17	
18	logger = init_logger(__name__)
19	
20	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
21	_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2
22	
23	
24	class SamplingType(IntEnum):
25	    GREEDY = 0
26	    RANDOM = 1
27	    RANDOM_SEED = 2
28	
29	
30	# maybe make msgspec?
31	@dataclass
32	class GuidedDecodingParams:
33	    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
34	    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
35	    regex: Optional[str] = None
36	    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
37	    grammar: Optional[str] = None
38	    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
39	    """These are other options that can be set"""
40	    backend: Optional[str] = None
41	    backend_was_auto: bool = False
42	    disable_fallback: bool = False
43	    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
44	    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
45	    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
46	    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
47	
48	    @staticmethod
49	    def from_optional(
50	        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
51	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
52	        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
53	        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
54	        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
55	        backend: Optional[str] = None,
56	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
57	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
58	    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
59	        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
60	                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
61	            return None
62	        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
63	        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
64	            json = json.model_json_schema()
65	        return GuidedDecodingParams(
66	            json=json,
67	            regex=regex,
68	            choice=choice,
69	            grammar=grammar,
70	            json_object=json_object,
71	            backend=backend,
72	            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
73	            structural_tag=structural_tag,
74	        )
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self):
77	        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
78	        guide_count = sum([
79	            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
80	            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
81	        ])
82	        if guide_count > 1:
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
85	                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")
86	
87	        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
88	            self._extract_backend_options()
89	
90	    @deprecated(
91	        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
92	        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
93	        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
94	        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
95	        "instead.")
96	    def _extract_backend_options(self):
97	        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
98	        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
99	        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
100	        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
101	        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
102	            self.disable_fallback = True
103	        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:
104	            self.disable_any_whitespace = True
105	        if "no-additional-properties" in options_set:
106	            self.disable_additional_properties = True
107	
108	
109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2
116	
117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        top_a: Float that filters tokens based on an adaptive threshold
155	            relative to the maximum token probability. Keeps only tokens whose
156	            probability is at least `top_a * max_probability`, then
157	            renormalizes. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
158	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
159	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
160	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
161	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
162	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
163	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
164	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
165	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
166	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
167	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
168	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
169	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
170	            can complete the sequence.
171	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
172	            output text. Defaults to False.
173	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
174	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
175	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
176	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
177	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
178	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
179	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
180	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
181	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
182	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
183	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
184	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
185	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
186	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
187	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
188	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
189	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
190	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
191	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
192	            a first argument.
193	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
194	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
195	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
196	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
197	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
198	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
199	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
200	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
201	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
202	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
203	            Defaults to None.
204	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
205	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
206	            sampling implementations.
207	    """
208	
209	    n: int = 1
210	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
211	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
212	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
213	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
214	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
215	    temperature: float = 1.0
216	    top_p: float = 1.0
217	    top_k: int = 0
218	    top_a: float = 0.0
219	    min_p: float = 0.0
220	    seed: Optional[int] = None
221	    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
222	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
223	    ignore_eos: bool = False
224	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
225	    min_tokens: int = 0
226	    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
227	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
228	    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
229	    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
230	    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
231	    detokenize: bool = True
232	    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
233	    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
234	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
235	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
236	    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
237	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
238	    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
239	    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
240	
241	    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
242	    # They are set in post_init.
243	    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
244	    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)
245	
246	    # Fields used to construct logits processors
247	    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
248	    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
249	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
250	    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
251	
252	    # Fields used for bad words
253	    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
254	    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
255	
256	    @staticmethod
257	    def from_optional(
258	        n: Optional[int] = 1,
259	        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
260	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
261	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
262	        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
263	        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
264	        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
265	        top_k: int = 0,
266	        top_a: float = 0.0,
267	        min_p: float = 0.0,
268	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
269	        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
270	        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
271	        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
272	        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
273	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
274	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
275	        min_tokens: int = 0,
276	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
277	        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
278	        detokenize: bool = True,
279	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
280	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
281	        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
282	        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
283	                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
284	        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
285	        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
286	        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
287	        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
288	        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
289	    ) -> "SamplingParams":
290	        if logit_bias is not None:
291	            # Convert token_id to integer
292	            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
293	            logit_bias = {
294	                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
295	                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
296	            }
297	
298	        return SamplingParams(
299	            n=1 if n is None else n,
300	            best_of=best_of,
301	            presence_penalty=0.0
302	            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
303	            frequency_penalty=0.0
304	            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
305	            repetition_penalty=1.0
306	            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
307	            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
308	            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
309	            top_k=top_k,
310	            top_a=top_a,
311	            min_p=min_p,
312	            seed=seed,
313	            stop=stop,
314	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
315	            bad_words=bad_words,
316	            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
317	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
318	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
319	            min_tokens=min_tokens,
320	            logprobs=logprobs,
321	            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
322	            detokenize=detokenize,
323	            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
324	            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
325	            logits_processors=logits_processors,
326	            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
327	            output_kind=output_kind,
328	            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
329	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
330	            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
331	            extra_args=extra_args,
332	        )
333	
334	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
335	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
336	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
337	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
338	        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
339	        # when we return the result, we will check
340	        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
341	        if self.best_of:
342	            if self.best_of < self.n:
343	                raise ValueError(
344	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
345	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
346	            if not self._real_n:
347	                self._real_n = self.n
348	                self.n = self.best_of
349	
350	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
351	            logger.warning(
352	                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
353	                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
354	                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
355	            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)
356	
357	        if self.seed == -1:
358	            self.seed = None
359	
360	        if self.stop is None:
361	            self.stop = []
362	        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
363	            self.stop = [self.stop]
364	
365	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
366	            self.stop_token_ids = []
367	
368	        if self.bad_words is None:
369	            self.bad_words = []
370	
371	        if self.logprobs is True:
372	            self.logprobs = 1
373	
374	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
375	            self.prompt_logprobs = 1
376	
377	        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
378	        # until sequence is finished.
379	        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
380	            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1
381	
382	        self._verify_args()
383	
384	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
385	            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
386	            self.top_p = 1.0
387	            self.top_k = 0
388	            self.top_a = 0.0
389	            self.min_p = 0.0
390	            self._verify_greedy_sampling()
391	
392	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
393	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
394	
395	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
396	        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
397	            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
398	                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
399	        if self.n < 1:
400	            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
401	        if self.best_of is not None:
402	            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
403	                raise ValueError(
404	                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
405	            if self.best_of < 1:
406	                raise ValueError(
407	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
408	            if self.best_of < self.n:
409	                raise ValueError(
410	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
411	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
412	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
413	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
414	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
415	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
416	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
417	                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
418	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
419	            raise ValueError(
420	                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
421	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
422	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
423	            raise ValueError(
424	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
425	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
426	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
427	        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
428	        if self.top_k < -1:
429	            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
430	                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
431	        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
432	            raise TypeError(
433	                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
434	        if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
435	            raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
436	                             f"{self.top_a}.")
437	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
438	            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
439	                             f"{self.min_p}.")
440	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
441	            raise ValueError(
442	                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
443	        if self.min_tokens < 0:
444	            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
445	                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
446	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
447	            raise ValueError(
448	                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
449	                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
450	        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
451	            raise ValueError(
452	                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
453	        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
454	            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
455	                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
456	        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
457	                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
458	            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
459	                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
460	        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
461	        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
462	            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
463	                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
464	        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
465	        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
466	            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
467	        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
468	            raise ValueError(
469	                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
470	                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
471	        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
472	                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
473	            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")
474	
475	    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
476	        if self.n > 1:
477	            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
478	                             f"got {self.n}.")
479	
480	    def update_from_generation_config(
481	            self,
482	            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
483	            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
484	        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""
485	
486	        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
487	            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
488	            # min_tokens processing.
489	            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)
490	
491	        # Update eos_token_id for generation
492	        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
493	            # it can be either int or list of int
494	            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
495	            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
496	                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
497	                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
498	                # purposes.
499	                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
500	            if eos_ids:
501	                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
502	                if not self.ignore_eos:
503	                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
504	                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)
505	
506	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
507	        if not self.bad_words:
508	            return
509	        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
510	        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
511	            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
512	            # and in the middle of text
513	            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
514	            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
515	                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
516	                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
517	                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
518	                                                    add_special_tokens=False)
519	
520	                # If no space at the beginning
521	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
522	                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
523	                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
524	                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
525	                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
526	                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
527	                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
528	
529	        invalid_token_ids = [
530	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
531	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
532	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
533	        ]
534	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
535	            raise ValueError(
536	                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
537	                f" but the following tokens"
538	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
539	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
540	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")
541	
542	    @cached_property
543	    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
544	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
545	            return SamplingType.GREEDY
546	        if self.seed is not None:
547	            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
548	        return SamplingType.RANDOM
549	
550	    @property
551	    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
552	        return self._all_stop_token_ids
553	
554	    @property
555	    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
556	        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
557	        return self._bad_words_token_ids
558	
559	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
560	        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.
561	
562	        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
563	        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
564	        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
565	        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
566	        """
567	
568	        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
569	            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
570	            for lp in self.logits_processors
571	        }
572	        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)
573	
574	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
575	        return (
576	            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
577	            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
578	            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
579	            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
580	            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
581	            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
582	            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
583	            f"top_a={self.top_a}, "
584	            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
585	            f"seed={self.seed}, "
586	            f"stop={self.stop}, "
587	            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
588	            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
589	            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
590	            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
591	            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
592	            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
593	            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
594	            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
595	            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
596	            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
597	            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
598	            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
599	            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
600	            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")
601

603	class BeamSearchParams(
604	        msgspec.Struct,
605	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]

10	from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams, SamplingType
11	from vllm.sequence import (VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE, SequenceData,
12	                           SequenceGroupMetadata)

14	                        is_pin_memory_available, make_tensor_with_pad)

16	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5

19	@dataclass
20	class SequenceGroupToSample:
21	    # |---------- N-1 iteration --------|
22	    # |---------------- N iteration ---------------------|
23	    # |- tokenA -|......................|-- newTokens ---|
24	    # |---------- context_len ----------|
25	    # |-------------------- seq_len ----------------------|
26	    #                                   |-- query_len ---|
27	
28	    # Sequence ids for the sequence group in a previous step.
29	    seq_ids: list[int]
30	    sampling_params: SamplingParams
31	    # seq_id -> sequence data.
32	    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
33	    # The length of the sequence (all tokens seen in the past + new token to
34	    # compute attention) of the sequence group. None if it is in a decode
35	    # stage.
36	    seq_len: Optional[int]
37	    # The length of new query tokens to compute in the current step. None if it
38	    # is in a decode stage. The length of query_len <= seq_len if chunked
39	    # prefill is enabled.
40	    query_len: Optional[int]
41	    # A random number generator for sampling.
42	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
43	    # True if the sequence group is in prefill stage. False if it is in a
44	    # decode stage.
45	    is_prompt: bool
46	    # Query token indices from logits. to compute prompt logprob. Empty if
47	    # prompt logprob is not required.
48	    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
49	    # Sample token indices from logits. Empty if sampling is not required.
50	    sample_indices: list[int]
51	
52	    @property
53	    def do_sample(self):
54	        return len(self.sample_indices) > 0
55	
56	    def __post_init__(self):
57	        if len(self.prompt_logprob_indices) > 0:
58	            assert self.sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None
59	        if self.is_prompt:
60	            assert self.seq_len is not None
61	            assert self.query_len is not None
62	
63	
64	def gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs: int):
65	    return lambda: SequenceGroupToSample(
66	        seq_ids=[0] * num_seqs,
67	        sampling_params=None,
68	        seq_data=None,  # type: ignore
69	        seq_len=0,
70	        query_len=0,
71	        generator=None,
72	        is_prompt=True,
73	        prompt_logprob_indices=[],
74	        sample_indices=[],
75	    )

78	class SamplingMetadataCache:
79	    """Used to cache SamplingMetadata objects between scheduler iterations"""
80	
81	    def __init__(self):
82	        self._seq_group_to_sample_cache: dict[int, PyObjectCache] = {}
83	
84	    def get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(self, num_seqs):
85	        if num_seqs not in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache:
86	            self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs] = PyObjectCache(
87	                gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs))
88	
89	        obj = self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs].get_object()
90	        return obj
91	
92	    def reset(self):
93	        for cache in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache.values():
94	            cache.reset()

96	
97	class SamplingMetadata:
98	    """Metadata for input sequences. Used in sampler.
99	
100	    The usage is as follow;
101	    ```
102	    hidden_states = execute_model(...)
103	    logits = hidden_states[sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices]
104	    sample(logits)
105	
106	    def sample(logits):
107	        # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling....
108	    ```
109	
110	    Args:
111	        seq_groups: List of batched sequence groups.
112	        selected_token_indices: (num_query_tokens_to_logprob). Indices to find
113	            logits from the initial model output hidden states.
114	        categorized_sample_indices: SamplingType -> token indices to sample.
115	            Each token indices is 2D tensor of (num_indices, num_indices) where
116	            the first item means the sample index within the returned logit
117	            (before pruning padding), and the second item means the sample
118	            index after pruning using selected_token_indices.
119	            For example, if the returned logit is [1, 2, 3], and we select
120	            [1, 2] for sampling, the pruned logit will be [2, 3]. In this case,
121	            The first tuple is [1, 2] (sampled index within original logit),
122	            and the second tuple is [0, 1] (sampled index within pruned logit).
123	        num_prompts: Number of prompt sequence groups in seq_groups.
124	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: Indicates if we want to skip the GPU=>CPU
125	            serialization of token outputs.
126	        reuse_sampling_tensors: Indicates if we want to reuse sampling
127	            tensors that are part of the sampler forward pass. Currently,
128	            it is mainly used for multi-step decode.
129	
130	    """
131	
132	    def __init__(
133	        self,
134	        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
135	        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
136	        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
137	        num_prompts: int,
138	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
139	        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
140	    ) -> None:
141	        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
142	        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
143	        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
144	        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
145	        self.skip_sampler_cpu_output = skip_sampler_cpu_output
146	        self.reuse_sampling_tensors = reuse_sampling_tensors
147	
148	    @staticmethod
149	    def prepare(
150	        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
151	        seq_lens: list[int],
152	        query_lens: list[int],
153	        device: str,
154	        pin_memory: bool,
155	        generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
156	        cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
157	    ) -> "SamplingMetadata":
158	        (
159	            seq_groups,
160	            selected_token_indices,
161	            categorized_sample_indices,
162	            num_prompts,
163	        ) = _prepare_seq_groups(seq_group_metadata_list, seq_lens, query_lens,
164	                                device, generators, cache)
165	        selected_token_indices = async_tensor_h2d(
166	            selected_token_indices,
167	            dtype=torch.long,
168	            target_device=device,
169	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
170	        )
171	        categorized_sample_indices = {
172	            t:
173	            async_tensor_h2d(
174	                seq_ids,
175	                dtype=torch.int,
176	                target_device=device,
177	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
178	            )
179	            for t, seq_ids in categorized_sample_indices.items()
180	        }
181	
182	        sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata(
183	            seq_groups=seq_groups,
184	            selected_token_indices=selected_token_indices,
185	            categorized_sample_indices=categorized_sample_indices,
186	            num_prompts=num_prompts,
187	        )
188	        return sampling_metadata
189	
190	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
191	        return (
192	            "SamplingMetadata("
193	            f"seq_groups={self.seq_groups}, "
194	            f"selected_token_indices={self.selected_token_indices}, "
195	            f"categorized_sample_indices={self.categorized_sample_indices})")
196	
197	
198	def _prepare_seq_groups(
199	    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
200	    seq_lens: list[int],
201	    query_lens: list[int],
202	    device: str,
203	    generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
204	    cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
205	) -> tuple[
206	        list[SequenceGroupToSample],
207	        list[int],
208	        dict[SamplingType, list[int]],
209	        int,
210	]:
211	    """Prepare sequence groups and indices for sampling.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        seq_group_metadata_list: A list of sequence group to batch.
215	        seq_lens: A list of sequence lens per sequence group.
216	            Index of prompt len should match with seq_group_metadata_list.
217	        query_lens: A list of query lengths. Prompt lens include the length
218	            of entire prompt tokens, and it could be shorter.
219	        device: A device to use for random number generators,
220	            `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`.
221	        generators: A store of per-request random number generators used
222	            for seeded requests.
223	
224	    Returns:
225	        seq_groups: A list of sequence group to sample.
226	        selected_token_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
227	        categorized_sample_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
228	        num_prompts: Total number of prompts from `seq_group_metadata_list`.
229	    """
230	    # Batched sequence groups for the current model forward stsep.
231	    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample] = []
232	    # A list of token indices to sample/compute logprob. It is used to
233	    # prune the outcome logits from the model for the performance.
234	    selected_token_indices: list[int] = []
235	    # Used for selected_token_indices.
236	    model_output_idx = 0
237	
238	    # Sampling type -> (
239	    # indices to sample/prompt logprob within pruned output logits,
240	    # indices to sample within pruned logits)
241	    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
242	        t: []
243	        for t in SamplingType
244	    }
245	    # Index of logits to compute logprob. Logits include both prompt logprob
246	    # and sample logprob indices.
247	    logit_idx = 0
248	    # Total number of prompts from given sequence groups.
249	    num_prompts = 0
250	
251	    for i, seq_group_metadata in enumerate(seq_group_metadata_list):
252	        seq_ids = seq_group_metadata.seq_data.keys()
253	
254	        if cache is not None:
255	            sample_obj = cache.get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(len(seq_ids))
256	
257	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
258	                sample_obj.seq_ids[j] = seq_id
259	
260	            sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices.clear()
261	            sample_obj.sample_indices.clear()
262	
263	        sampling_params = seq_group_metadata.sampling_params
264	        is_prompt = seq_group_metadata.is_prompt
265	        generator: Optional[torch.Generator] = None
266	        # If the current seq group is in decode stage, it is None.
267	        seq_len: Optional[int] = None
268	        query_len: Optional[int] = None
269	        prompt_logprob_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices
270	                                             if cache is not None else [])
271	        sample_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.sample_indices
272	                                     if cache is not None else [])
273	        do_sample = seq_group_metadata.do_sample
274	
275	        if seq_group_metadata.is_prompt:
276	            if sampling_params.seed is not None:
277	                generator = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(
278	                    sampling_params.seed)
279	                if generators is not None:
280	                    generators[seq_group_metadata.request_id] = generator
281	
282	            num_prompts += 1
283	            num_prefill_sample = len(seq_ids)
284	            assert num_prefill_sample == 1
285	            assert query_lens is not None and seq_lens is not None
286	            query_len, seq_len = query_lens[i], seq_lens[i]
287	            # If we need sampling, exclude num_prefill_sample tokens from
288	            # prompt logprob.
289	            prompt_logprob_len = (query_len - num_prefill_sample
290	                                  if do_sample else query_len)
291	            sample_len = num_prefill_sample if do_sample else 0
292	        else:
293	            # Decode
294	            prompt_logprob_len = 0
295	            query_len = query_lens[i] if query_lens is not None and len(
296	                query_lens) > 0 else 1
297	            sample_len = len(seq_ids) * query_len if do_sample else 0
298	
299	            if sampling_params.seed is not None and generators is not None:
300	                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)
301	
302	        # Update indices to select from the model output.
303	        """
304	        This blocks computes selected_token_indices which is used in the
305	        following way.
306	
307	        hidden_states = model(...)
308	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
309	        """
310	
311	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
312	            selected_token_indices.extend(
313	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
314	        model_output_idx += prompt_logprob_len
315	        if do_sample:
316	            selected_token_indices.extend(
317	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + sample_len))
318	        model_output_idx += sample_len
319	
320	        # We now find indices for logprob computation and sampling.
321	        """
322	        This block computes categorized_sample_indices which is used in the
323	        following way.
324	
325	        hidden_states = model(...)
326	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
327	        def sample(logits):
328	           # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling.
329	           # prompt_logprob_indices to find prompt logprob indices.
330	           # sample_indices to find sample indices.
331	        """
332	
333	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
334	            prompt_logprob_indices.extend(
335	                range(logit_idx, logit_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
336	            logit_idx += prompt_logprob_len
337	        if do_sample:
338	            sample_indices.extend(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len))
339	            categorized_sample_indices[sampling_params.sampling_type].extend(
340	                list(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len)))
341	            logit_idx += sample_len
342	
343	        if cache is not None:
344	            sample_obj.sampling_params = sampling_params
345	            sample_obj.seq_data = seq_group_metadata.seq_data
346	            sample_obj.seq_len = seq_len
347	            sample_obj.query_len = query_len
348	            sample_obj.generator = generator
349	            sample_obj.is_prompt = is_prompt
350	        else:
351	            sample_obj = SequenceGroupToSample(
352	                seq_ids=list(seq_ids),
353	                sampling_params=sampling_params,
354	                seq_data=seq_group_metadata.seq_data,
355	                seq_len=seq_len,
356	                query_len=query_len,
357	                generator=generator,
358	                is_prompt=is_prompt,
359	                prompt_logprob_indices=list(prompt_logprob_indices),
360	                sample_indices=list(sample_indices),
361	            )
362	
363	        seq_groups.append(sample_obj)
364	
365	    if cache is not None:
366	        cache.reset()
367	
368	    return (seq_groups, selected_token_indices, categorized_sample_indices,
369	            num_prompts)
370	
371	
372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    top_as: torch.Tensor
380	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
381	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
384	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
386	
387	    @classmethod
388	    def from_sampling_metadata(
389	        cls,
390	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
391	        vocab_size: int,
392	        device: torch.device,
393	        dtype: torch.dtype,
394	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool, bool]:
395	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
397	        top_ks: list[int] = []
398	        temperatures: list[float] = []
399	        top_ps: list[float] = []
400	        top_as: list[float] = []
401	        min_ps: list[float] = []
402	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
404	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
405	        do_penalties = False
406	        do_top_p_top_k = False
407	        do_top_a = False
408	        do_min_p = False
409	
410	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
411	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
412	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
413	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
414	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
415	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
416	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
417	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
418	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
419	            top_a = sampling_params.top_a
420	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
421	
422	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
423	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
424	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
425	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
426	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
427	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
428	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
429	                temperature = 1.0
430	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
431	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
432	                do_top_p_top_k = True
433	            if not do_top_a and top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS:
434	                do_top_a = True
435	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
436	                do_min_p = True
437	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
438	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
439	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
440	                do_penalties = True
441	
442	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
443	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
444	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
445	                # their logprobs
446	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
447	                assert query_len is not None
448	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
449	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
450	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
451	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
452	                top_as += [top_a] * prefill_len
453	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
454	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
455	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
456	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
457	
458	            if seq_group.do_sample:
459	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
460	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
461	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
462	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
463	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
464	                top_as += [top_a] * sample_lens
465	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
466	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
467	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
468	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
469	
470	        if do_penalties:
471	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
472	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
473	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
474	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
475	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
476	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
477	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
478	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
479	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
480	                    output_tokens.extend(
481	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
482	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
483	                if seq_group.do_sample:
484	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
485	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
486	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
487	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
488	
489	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
490	            temperatures,
491	            top_ps,
492	            top_ks,
493	            top_as,
494	            min_ps,
495	            presence_penalties,
496	            frequency_penalties,
497	            repetition_penalties,
498	            prompt_tokens,
499	            output_tokens,
500	            vocab_size,
501	            device,
502	            dtype,
503	        )
504	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p,
505	                do_top_a)
506	
507	    @classmethod
508	    def from_lists(
509	        cls,
510	        temperatures: list[float],
511	        top_ps: list[float],
512	        top_ks: list[int],
513	        top_as: list[float],
514	        min_ps: list[float],
515	        presence_penalties: list[float],
516	        frequency_penalties: list[float],
517	        repetition_penalties: list[float],
518	        prompt_tokens: list[array],
519	        output_tokens: list[array],
520	        vocab_size: int,
521	        device: torch.device,
522	        dtype: torch.dtype,
523	    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
524	        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
525	        # pinned memory.
526	        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
527	
528	        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens
529	
530	        if do_penalties:
531	            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
532	                prompt_tokens,
533	                vocab_size,
534	                device="cpu",
535	                dtype=torch.int64,
536	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
537	            )
538	            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
539	                output_tokens,
540	                vocab_size,
541	                device="cpu",
542	                dtype=torch.int64,
543	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
544	            )
545	        else:
546	            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
547	            prompt_t = empty_tensor
548	            output_t = empty_tensor
549	
550	        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
551	            temperatures,
552	            device="cpu",
553	            dtype=dtype,
554	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
555	        )
556	        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
557	            top_ps,
558	            device="cpu",
559	            dtype=dtype,
560	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
561	        )
562	        top_as_t = torch.tensor(
563	            top_as,
564	            device="cpu",
565	            dtype=dtype,
566	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
567	        )
568	        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
569	            min_ps,
570	            device="cpu",
571	            dtype=dtype,
572	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
573	        )
574	        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
575	            presence_penalties,
576	            device="cpu",
577	            dtype=dtype,
578	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
579	        )
580	        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
581	            frequency_penalties,
582	            device="cpu",
583	            dtype=dtype,
584	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
585	        )
586	        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
587	            repetition_penalties,
588	            device="cpu",
589	            dtype=dtype,
590	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
591	        )
592	        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
593	            top_ks,
594	            device="cpu",
595	            dtype=torch.int,
596	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
597	        )
598	        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
599	        # transfer to device.
600	
601	        return cls(
602	            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
603	            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
604	            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
605	            top_as=top_as_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
606	            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
607	            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
608	                                                       non_blocking=True),
609	            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
610	                                                         non_blocking=True),
611	            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
612	                                                           non_blocking=True),
613	            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
614	            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
615	        )
616

4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	from typing import Optional
6	
7	import torch
8	
9	from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import LogitsProcessorManager
10	
11	
12	@dataclass
13	class SamplingMetadata:
14	
15	    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
16	    all_greedy: bool
17	    all_random: bool
18	
19	    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
20	    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]
21	    top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]
22	
23	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]
24	
25	    # None means no logprobs, 0 means sampled token logprobs only
26	    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]
27	
28	    no_penalties: bool
29	    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
30	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
31	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
32	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
33	
34	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]
35	
36	    # `allowed_token_ids_mask` is a 2D bool tensor of shape (max batch size,
37	    # vocab size).
38	    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]
39	
40	    # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
41	    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]
42	
43	    # Loaded logits processors
44	    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
14	
15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
16	
17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top-a filtering before top_k/top_p.
121	        if sampling_metadata.top_a is not None:
122	            logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_metadata.top_a)
123	
124	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
125	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
126	            logits,
127	            sampling_metadata.generators,
128	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
129	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
130	        )
131	
132	        if greedy_sampled is None:
133	            return random_sampled
134	
135	        sampled = torch.where(
136	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
137	            greedy_sampled,
138	            random_sampled,
139	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
140	        )
141	        return sampled
142	
143	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
144	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
145	
146	    def gather_logprobs(
147	        self,
148	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
149	        num_logprobs: int,
150	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
151	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
152	        """
153	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
154	
155	        Args:
156	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
157	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
158	                        retain per token
159	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
160	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
161	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
162	                     with (num tokens) elements
163	                     Must be int64.
164	
165	        Returns:
166	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
167	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
168	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
169	        """
170	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
171	        # Find the topK values.
172	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
173	                                                 num_logprobs,
174	                                                 dim=-1)
175	
176	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
177	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
178	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
179	
180	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
181	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
182	
183	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
184	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
185	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
186	
187	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
188	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
189	
190	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
191	
192	    def apply_penalties(
193	        self,
194	        logits: torch.Tensor,
195	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
196	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
197	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
198	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
199	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
200	                logits,
201	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
202	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
203	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
204	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
205	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
206	            )
207	        return logits
208	
209	    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
210	        self,
211	        logits: torch.Tensor,
212	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
213	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
214	        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
215	            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
216	                                float("-inf"))
217	        return logits
218	
219	    def apply_bad_words(
220	        self,
221	        logits: torch.Tensor,
222	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
223	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
224	        if sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids:
225	            apply_bad_words(
226	                logits,
227	                sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids,
228	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
229	            )
230	        return logits
231	
232	
233	def _apply_top_a(
234	    logits: torch.Tensor,
235	    top_a: torch.Tensor,
236	) -> torch.Tensor:
237	    """Apply Top-A sampling to logits.
238	
239	    Top-A keeps only tokens whose probability is at least
240	    `top_a * max_probability`, then masks the rest so they are not
241	    sampled. When top_a is 0.0, no filtering is applied.
242	    """
243	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
244	    max_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
245	    threshold = top_a.unsqueeze(dim=1) * max_probs
246	    tokens_to_remove = probs < threshold
247	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
248	    return logits

86	class SamplerOutput(
87	        msgspec.Struct,
88	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
89	        array_like=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
90	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
91	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
92	
93	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
94	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
95	    """
96	
97	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]
98	
99	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.
100	    sampled_token_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
101	
102	    # On-device tensor containing the logprobs of each token.
103	    logprobs: Optional["torch.Tensor"] = None
104	
105	    # Holds either (1) the pythonized sampler result (single-step scheduling)
106	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the
107	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)
108	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.
111	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
114	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
116	
117	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
118	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are
119	    # specified in lieu of prompt token ids or text.
120	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
121	
122	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.
123	    spec_decode_worker_metrics: Optional[SpecDecodeWorkerMetrics] = None
124	
125	    # Optional last hidden states from the model.
126	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
127	
128	    # Optional prefill hidden states from the model
129	    # (used for models like EAGLE).
130	    prefill_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
131	
132	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers
133	    model_forward_time: Optional[float] = None
134	
135	    # Time taken in the model execute function. This will include model forward,
136	    # block/sync across workers, cpu-gpu sync time and sampling time.
137	    model_execute_time: Optional[float] = None
138	
139	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
140	        return self.outputs[idx]
141	
142	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):
143	        self.outputs[idx] = value
144	
145	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]:
146	        return iter(self.outputs)
147	
148	    def __len__(self):
149	        return len(self.outputs)
150	
151	    def __eq__(self, other: object):
152	        return isinstance(other,
153	                          self.__class__) and self.outputs == other.outputs
154	
155	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
156	        """Show the shape of a tensor instead of its values to reduce noise.
157	        """
158	        sampled_token_probs_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_probs is None
159	                                    else self.sampled_token_probs.shape)
160	        sampled_token_ids_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_ids is None else
161	                                  self.sampled_token_ids.shape)
162	        return (
163	            f"SamplerOutput(outputs={self.outputs}, "
164	            f"sampled_token_probs={sampled_token_probs_repr}, "
165	            f"sampled_token_ids={sampled_token_ids_repr}, "
166	            f"spec_decode_worker_metrics={self.spec_decode_worker_metrics})")

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    5a. Apply min-p and top-a filtering.
180	    6. Sample the next tokens.
181	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
182	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
183	
184	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
185	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
186	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
187	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
188	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
189	    """
190	
191	    def __init__(self):
192	        super().__init__()
193	
194	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
195	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
196	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
197	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
198	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
199	
200	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
